## Claims

### abstract-class-cannot-be-instantiated [IN] OBSERVATION
Abstract classes cannot be instantiated directly and may contain abstract methods; a pure abstract base class in C++ containing only pure virtual methods is equivalent to an interface
- Source: entries/2026/06/19/wiki-Class_computer_programming.md
- Source URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Class_(computer_programming)

### abstract-factory-creates-families [IN] OBSERVATION
The Abstract Factory pattern provides an interface for creating families of related or dependent objects without specifying their concrete classes, distinguishing it from Factory Method which creates single objects.
- Source: entries/2026/06/19/wiki-Abstract_factory_pattern.md
- Source URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abstract_factory_pattern

### abstract-factory-exemplifies-composition-and-dip [IN] DERIVED
Abstract Factory illustrates both composition-over-inheritance and DIP: it uses composition (client depends on abstract factory interface, not concrete classes), the abstraction can be owned by the high-level policy layer consistent with DIP, and swapping product families requires changing only which concrete factory is instantiated rather than every instantiation site.
- Source type: derived
- Depends on: abstract-factory-uses-composition, abstract-factory-swap-one-line, dip-abstractions-owned-by-high-level-layer

### abstract-factory-implementable-via-prototype-or-factory-method [IN] OBSERVATION
Abstract Factory can be implemented via either Factory Method (inheritance) or Prototype (delegation/cloning)
- Source: entries/2026/06/19/wiki-Prototype_pattern.md
- Source URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prototype_pattern

### abstract-factory-implementation-encodes-composition-axis [IN] DERIVED
Abstract Factory's dual implementation path — via Factory Method (inheritance-based subclassing) or Prototype (delegation/cloning) — encodes the inheritance-to-composition axis within a single pattern's implementation options, making the choice of implementation mechanism itself a microcosm of the broader OO evolution trajectory.
- Source type: derived
- Depends on: abstract-factory-implementable-via-prototype-or-factory-method, factory-method-relies-on-inheritance, prototype-creates-objects-by-cloning

### abstract-factory-most-principle-dense-pattern [IN] DERIVED
Abstract Factory satisfies multiple design principles simultaneously: composition-over-inheritance (client depends on abstract factory interface, not concrete classes), dependency inversion (the abstraction is owned by the high-level policy layer), and it addresses class proliferation in product family creation. Combined with Bridge's role in solving M×N dimensionality problems through composition, these patterns illustrate how composition-based patterns tend to satisfy multiple SOLID principles. However, the antecedents do not directly establish Abstract Factory's internal mechanism for reducing dimensionality, nor do they support a comparative ranking of principle-density across all GoF patterns.
- Source type: derived
- Depends on: abstract-factory-exemplifies-composition-and-dip, bridge-and-abstract-factory-solve-dimensionality-problem

### abstract-factory-often-singleton [IN] OBSERVATION
Abstract factories are often implemented as singletons — a single global factory object through which all client code routes creation requests.
- Source: entries/2026/06/19/wiki-Abstract_factory_pattern.md
- Source URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abstract_factory_pattern

### abstract-factory-participants [IN] OBSERVATION
The Abstract Factory pattern has five structural participants: AbstractFactory, ConcreteFactory, AbstractProduct, ConcreteProduct, and Client.
- Source: entries/2026/06/19/wiki-Abstract_factory_pattern.md
- Source URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abstract_factory_pattern

### abstract-factory-singleton-coupling-reveals-di-advantage [IN] DERIVED
Abstract Factory's typical implementation as a Singleton creates a transitive dependency on Singleton's anti-pattern characteristics (global state, hidden coupling, testing difficulty) — a coupling that DI naturally eliminates by making the factory injectable, illustrating how the creational pattern family's internal evolution from Singleton to DI improves even the most principle-dense patterns.
- Source type: derived
- Depends on: abstract-factory-often-singleton, di-replaces-singleton-as-global-access-mechanism, abstract-factory-most-principle-dense-pattern

### abstract-factory-swap-one-line [IN] OBSERVATION
Changing the product family in an Abstract Factory requires changing only which concrete factory is instantiated — typically one line in one file — rather than changing every instantiation site.
- Source: entries/2026/06/19/wiki-Abstract_factory_pattern.md
- Source URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abstract_factory_pattern

### abstract-factory-uses-composition [IN] OBSERVATION
The Abstract Factory pattern relies on object composition rather than inheritance of the client class — client code depends only on abstract interfaces, never on concrete classes.
- Source: entries/2026/06/19/wiki-Abstract_factory_pattern.md
- Source URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abstract_factory_pattern

### abstract-type-cannot-instantiate [IN] OBSERVATION
An abstract type cannot be instantiated directly; instantiation occurs only through a concrete subtype that implements all abstract methods.
- Source: entries/2026/06/19/wiki-Abstract_type.md
- Source URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abstract_type

### abstract-type-vs-adt-distinction [IN] OBSERVATION
Abstract types and abstract data types (ADTs) are different concepts: an abstract type is about instantiation and subtyping, while an ADT is about encapsulation and information hiding.
- Source: entries/2026/06/19/wiki-Abstract_type.md
- Source URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abstract_type

### abstractness-propagates-to-subclass [IN] OBSERVATION
A subclass that inherits from an abstract type but does not implement all inherited abstract methods is itself abstract.
- Source: entries/2026/06/19/wiki-Abstract_type.md
- Source URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abstract_type

### abstractness-propagation-self-enforces-hierarchy-discipline [IN] DERIVED
The propagation of abstractness to subclasses (a subclass inheriting abstract methods without implementing all of them is itself abstract) provides a type-system enforcement mechanism for the hierarchy discipline: the non-leaf-abstract plus final/sealed heuristic is not merely advisory but self-reinforcing, because the type system automatically prevents incomplete implementations from appearing at leaf positions where they could be instantiated.
- Source type: derived
- Depends on: abstractness-propagates-to-subclass, final-sealed-and-non-leaf-abstract-complete-hierarchy-discipline

### access-control-is-visibility-independent-layer [IN] DERIVED
Access specifiers (private/protected/public) control usability rather than visibility — a private member may be visible but unusable — establishing access control as an independent layer from information hiding: access control restricts what code can do with visible elements, while information hiding restricts what elements are visible at all, and effective encapsulation requires both layers working in concert.
- Source type: derived
- Depends on: access-specifiers-not-visibility, info-hiding-hides-design-decisions-not-just-data

### access-specifiers-not-visibility [IN] OBSERVATION
Access specifiers (private, protected, public) do not necessarily control visibility — a private member may be visible to client code but not usable by it
- Source: entries/2026/06/19/wiki-Class_computer_programming.md
- Source URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Class_(computer_programming)

### acyclic-dependencies-principle-dag [IN] OBSERVATION
The Acyclic Dependencies Principle requires that the dependency graph of packages/components form a directed acyclic graph (DAG) with no cycles.
- Source: entries/2026/06/19/wiki-Object-oriented_design.md
- Source URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Object-oriented_design

### adapter-bridge-preventive-vs-corrective-interface-mediation [IN] DERIVED
Adapter and Bridge represent corrective and preventive modes of the same compositional interface mediation concern: Adapter's three-role structure (Client, Adaptee, Target) reifies existing interface incompatibility as explicit architectural debt, while Bridge, designed upfront, prevents that incompatibility from arising by separating abstraction from implementation before interfaces diverge.
- Source type: derived
- Depends on: bridge-designed-upfront-adapter-applied-after, adapter-three-roles

### adapter-object-vs-class-variant [IN] OBSERVATION
The Adapter pattern has two structural variants: object adapter (uses composition/delegation, can adapt an adaptee and all its subclasses) and class adapter (uses multiple inheritance, adapts only a specific concrete class).
- Source: entries/2026/06/19/wiki-Adapter_pattern.md
- Source URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Adapter_pattern

### adapter-three-roles [IN] OBSERVATION
The Adapter pattern has three roles: Client (needs the target interface), Adaptee (has useful functionality but wrong interface), and Adapter (bridges the two by wrapping the adaptee).
- Source: entries/2026/06/19/wiki-Adapter_pattern.md
- Source URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Adapter_pattern

### adapter-vs-decorator-vs-facade-vs-proxy [IN] OBSERVATION
Adapter converts an interface (different interface out); Decorator adds behavior but preserves the same interface; Facade simplifies/creates a new interface to a subsystem; Proxy provides the same interface but controls access.
- Source: entries/2026/06/19/wiki-Adapter_pattern.md
- Source URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Adapter_pattern

### adhoc-polymorphism-not-fundamental-type-system-feature [IN] OBSERVATION
Ad hoc polymorphism (function/operator overloading) is not a fundamental feature of the type system — the compiler treats overloaded functions as entirely distinct functions.
- Source: entries/2026/06/19/wiki-Polymorphism_computer_science.md

### alexander-arc-is-self-correcting-historical-process [IN] DERIVED
The full Alexander-to-dissolution arc — from architectural patterns addressing timeless spatial needs through GoF's language-contingent adaptation to the catalog's self-consuming dissolution — may function not merely as a passive historical narrative but as a partially self-steering process: the composition-over-inheritance axis appears to simultaneously drive the catalog toward dissolution (self-consuming) and index the severity of the foundational error being compensated for (self-correcting), suggesting the arc has self-steering characteristics rather than being merely observed, though the precise structural mechanism linking the self-consuming and self-correcting properties remains underspecified.
- Source type: derived
- Depends on: alexander-to-dissolution-complete-arc, composition-axis-self-consuming-and-self-correcting

### alexander-gof-translation-changed-pattern-ontology [IN] DERIVED
Design patterns originated from Christopher Alexander's architectural pattern language (1977) and were adapted to software. The GoF catalog preserved the form (named solutions to recurring problems) but changed the nature of the patterns: while Alexander's patterns addressed architectural concerns, the GoF patterns function as language-contingent workarounds for type-system deficiencies that dissolve when language features remove the underlying limitation. This suggests the cross-domain translation shifted patterns from broader design principles toward ephemeral compensations for specific language constraints.
- Source type: derived
- Depends on: design-patterns-originated-christopher-alexander-1977, gof-patterns-are-language-contingent

### alexander-to-dissolution-complete-arc [IN] DERIVED
The full arc from Alexander's architectural patterns (timeless spatial needs) through GoF's adaptation (language-contingent workarounds) to the catalog's self-consuming dissolution traces a complete ontological trajectory: patterns began as aspirationally universal, were discovered to be contingent upon translation to software, and ultimately encode the terms of their own obsolescence.
- Source type: derived
- Depends on: alexander-gof-translation-changed-pattern-ontology, composition-axis-self-consuming-and-self-correcting

AOP independently confirms from outside the GoF catalog what the catalog encodes internally: pattern contingency and total dissolution. ### aop-independently-validates-pattern-contingency [IN] DERIVED
AOP provides independent validation of GoF pattern contingency from two directions: cross-cutting concerns that resist standard OO decomposition motivate aspect-oriented solutions, and Hannemann & Kiczales empirically demonstrated that AspectJ removes code-level dependencies from 17 of 23 GoF patterns — confirming that pattern dependencies are artifacts of language limitations, not inherent design needs.
- Source type: derived

### behavioral-extensibility-complete-but-contingent [IN] DERIVED
GoF behavioral patterns form a layered extensibility architecture — from foundational mechanisms (Template Method for IoC, Strategy for composition-based extension) through collaborative clusters (Command+Memento, Composite+Iterator+Visitor) — but this architecture is historically bounded because the pattern mechanisms themselves are language-contingent workarounds that simplify or vanish in richer type systems.
- Source type: derived
- Depends on: behavioral-patterns-layer-from-mechanisms-to-clusters, ocp-mechanisms-are-language-contingent

### behavioral-pattern-count-reflects-dispatch-compensation-burden [IN] DERIVED
The behavioral category is the largest among the GoF patterns (11 of 23). Since behavioral patterns address concerns like dispatch routing, control flow, and state management — areas where single-dispatch type systems limit expressiveness compared to multiple-dispatch systems — this concentration may partly reflect the degree of pattern-level workaround needed to compensate for dispatching on only the receiver's type.
- Source type: derived
- Depends on: gof-eleven-behavioral-patterns, single-dispatch-vs-multiple-dispatch

### behavioral-patterns-form-collaborative-clusters [IN] DERIVED
GoF behavioral patterns operate as collaborative clusters rather than independent solutions: Command+Memento form a complete undo architecture (operation capture + state capture), while Iterator and Visitor both traverse Composite structures (sequential access + operation dispatch) — the behavioral category is internally compositional, with patterns designed for pairing.
- Source type: derived
- Depends on: command-memento-complete-undo-architecture, composite-structural-foundation-for-behavioral-patterns

### behavioral-patterns-layer-from-mechanisms-to-clusters [IN] DERIVED
GoF behavioral patterns exhibit two complementary organizational forces: foundational mechanisms that serve dual roles in framework architecture and extensibility (Template Method for IoC, Factory Method bridging IoC and OCP, Strategy for composition-based extensibility), and collaborative domain clusters where patterns are designed for pairing (Command+Memento for undo, Composite+Iterator+Visitor for tree operations) — suggesting the behavioral category has internal compositional structure rather than being a flat collection of independent solutions.
- Source type: derived
- Depends on: behavioral-patterns-form-collaborative-clusters, extensibility-and-ioc-share-pattern-mechanisms

### behavioral-reification-belongs-in-dissolving-partition [IN] DERIVED
The behavioral reification meta-pattern shared by Command, State, and Strategy — converting implicit procedural control flow into explicit first-class objects — is a type-system compensation that belongs squarely in the dissolving partition: languages with first-class functions, closures, and pattern matching render this object-level reification unnecessary, dissolving Command into lambdas, State into algebraic data types, and Strategy into higher-order functions.
- Source type: derived
- Depends on: behavioral-reification-unifies-command-state-strategy, oo-knowledge-partitions-into-dissolving-and-enduring

### behavioral-reification-dissolves-because-languages-absorb-features [IN] DERIVED
Behavioral reification's placement in the dissolving partition is causally explained by Command's status as the paradigmatic case of a pattern compensating for a missing language feature (first-class functions): behavioral reification dissolves precisely because languages absorb the features that motivate it — the reification was never an architectural insight but a workaround for insufficient language expressiveness.
- Source type: derived
- Depends on: behavioral-reification-belongs-in-dissolving-partition, command-paradigmatic-case-of-pattern-as-missing-feature

### behavioral-reification-unifies-command-state-strategy [IN] DERIVED
Command, State, and Strategy share a meta-pattern of behavioral reification — converting implicit procedural control flow into explicit first-class objects: Command reifies method invocations (enabling undo, queuing, logging), State reifies FSM transitions (enabling runtime behavioral type-change), and Strategy reifies algorithm selection (enabling runtime switching) — all achieving the same structural transformation from conditional branching to compositional delegation.
- Source type: derived
- Depends on: command-pattern-encapsulates-request-as-object, state-pattern-partial-runtime-type-change, state-vs-strategy-transition-control-difference

### bridge-and-abstract-factory-solve-dimensionality-problem [IN] DERIVED
Bridge and Abstract Factory both address class proliferation problems using composition over inheritance. Bridge solves the M × N class explosion by separating abstraction and implementation dimensions into M + N classes through delegation. Abstract Factory addresses product family creation by providing an interface for creating families of related objects without specifying concrete classes. While both patterns reduce subclass proliferation, the antecedents only directly establish composition/delegation as Bridge's primary decoupling mechanism — Abstract Factory's internal mechanism is not specified by the evidence.
- Source type: derived
- Depends on: bridge-solves-class-explosion, abstract-factory-creates-families, bridge-uses-composition-not-inheritance

### builder-abstract-factory-complementary-creational-spectrum [IN] DERIVED
Builder and Abstract Factory address different aspects of object creation: Abstract Factory creates families of related objects in one step, while Builder constructs one complex object step-by-step. ### closed-structure-predicts-specific-post-dissolution-vocabulary [IN] DERIVED
The closed epistemic structure of OO knowledge — where inheritance error as the single causal engine generates both the compensatory ecosystem and its dissolution terminus — combined with the dissolution fixed point's specific concept survival predictions, yields a complete predictive theory: the type-system error that generates the knowledge system also determines exactly which concepts persist after dissolution, from delegation forms to encapsulation stacks, making the post-dissolution design vocabulary fully determined by the pre-dissolution error.

### cohesion-and-coupling-define-complete-module-design-space [IN] DERIVED
SRP and ISP formalize cohesion (what belongs together inside a module) across complementary levels — internal responsibilities and external interface exposure — while structural wrapper patterns (Adapter, Decorator, Facade, Proxy) formalize coupling reduction through delegation and Law of Demeter enforcement. Together these address module design from both the internal-structure and boundary-communication perspectives, connecting Constantine-era cohesion and coupling concepts to modern SOLID principles and GoF patterns.
- Source type: derived

### cohesion-formalization-establishes-bounded-range [IN] DERIVED
SRP and ISP's complete formalization of cohesion defines an upper bound (don't combine unrelated responsibilities or expose unused methods), while the heuristic that high cohesion does not mean single-method classes establishes a lower bound — proper module design requires finding a bounded range of cohesion, not maximizing it, where both over-splitting and under-splitting are formalized as design errors.

The creational pattern space addresses both sequential and combinatorial complexity: Builder constructs a single complex object step-by-step (depth-first creation), while Abstract Factory creates families of related objects in one step (breadth-first creation), together covering the full dimensionality of complex object creation. Bridge complements these by reducing M × N class explosion to M + N through separating abstraction and implementation dimensions via delegation. However, the evidence does not establish that these patterns provide complete coverage of all fundamental scaling dimensions in object creation — only that they address complementary forms of creational complexity.

The SoC → structured design → SOLID → GoF intellectual genealogy progressively refines design guidance from abstract principle to concrete mechanism, where each layer operationalizes the one above. ### interpreter-exemplifies-composite-dependent-dissolution [IN] DERIVED
The Interpreter pattern's limitations (best suited for simple, stable grammars; defers to Visitor or parser generators for complex cases) combined with its structural dependence on Composite (the AST it evaluates is a Composite instance) suggest that Interpreter is a strong candidate for a pattern whose relevance diminishes alongside its structural host: since Composite's core design difficulties — the transparency/safety tension and the expression problem — are type-system artifacts that dissolve with richer type systems, Interpreter's foundation weakens when Composite's rationale does, though other factors beyond structural dependence may also influence Interpreter's continued utility.

### structural-patterns-span-three-architectural-functions [IN] DERIVED
The seven GoF structural patterns serve at least three distinct architectural functions: four wrapper patterns (Adapter, Decorator, Facade, Proxy) form a coherent subsystem unified by delegation and Law of Demeter enforcement to mediate interfaces and reduce coupling; Flyweight bridges object and value semantics by splitting intrinsic (shared, value-like) and extrinsic (per-use, object-like) state; and Bridge addresses dimensional class explosion by separating abstraction from implementation using composition (with Abstract Factory solving the same M×N problem from the creation side). This suggests the structural category spans interface management, memory/identity optimization, and combinatorial complexity reduction, though Composite and the remaining structural pattern are not accounted for in this grouping.

### visitor-pattern-uses-double-dispatch [IN] OBSERVATION
The Visitor pattern simulates double dispatch in single-dispatch languages: element.accept(visitor) dispatches on element type, then visitor.visit(this) dispatches on visitor type.

### wrapper-taxonomy-governed-by-two-orthogonal-dimensions [IN] DERIVED
